passage.title: Story Exhibit Sign Project
passage.text: The display team wanted children to follow a history wall in the right order. At first, the group posted a colorful wall that mixed old objects and directions together, but children liked the pictures but missed the order of events. Museum guides said "The display is pretty, yet I still skip a step in the timeline." After hearing that, the team placed a start arrow and end arrow at the clearest points and added one short question under every event card. They also placed a check-your-order card beside the final exhibit so the final card let them check whether they followed the whole story. On the next test day, children followed the history wall more steadily during the second test. The team noticed that clear sequence clues mattered more than extra decoration. Because of that result, helpers chose to use the new timeline format in other story displays.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Making a story exhibit easier for children to understand
Painting a room with brighter colors
Buying more books for a library sale
Writing long reports about class rules
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: The labels were too small to read quickly
The first plan had too many steps
Students could not tell what to do next
Children missed the order of events
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the group place beside the final exhibit?
choices: Short signs
Color blocks
A check-your-order card
Larger arrows
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does retold mean in the sixth sentence?
choices: A person who leads the school
Told again in your own words
A room that is easy to clean
A rule about lunch time
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: Shorter event cards reduced confusion about the timeline
It made the room much larger
It reduced the number of students
It removed every sign from the area
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Use the same clearer style in more places
Remove every sign from the room
Ask students to memorize everything
Apply the new timeline format to other story walls
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
